rulings.Case Title: WING WO MA VS. JUN TAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendants Kevin Curran, Curran &. Associates, Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2012, Line 20, Cross Defendants Kevin Curran, Curran &. Associates, Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication Denied in its entirety. Regarding the conspiracy and aiding and abetting issues, Cross-defendants have not satisfied their initial burden of production. Simply stating that Cross-complainants have no evidence is not sufficient to satisfy the burden. There are triable issues regarding the individual liability of Kevin Curran. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: MAYNARD GARCIA et al VS. PEAK ATTRACTIONS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Complainant Rebound Unlimited, Inc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2012, Line 21, Cross Complainant Rebound Unlimited, Inc'S Motion For Summary Judgment The motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. There are triable issues whether defendant Rebound Unlmited: 1) had a duty to warn about the possibility that the pole could break, 2) sold the pole that broke, 3) assumed liability for the pole during the dissolution of Rebound Action Sports, and 4) is a corporate successor of Rebound Action Sports. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO PLASCENCIA VS. ALL SEAS WHOLESALE INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel And Req For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 6, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF ALFREDO PLASCENCIA'S Motion To Compel And Request For Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel as to RFPD Nos. 1, 2, 3, 5-10, 12-14, 16, 18 and Interrogatory No. 24 is granted unless Defendant can bring to the hearing supplemental responses that were served before the motion to compel was filed. Any responsive documents must be produced by September 21, 2012. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $750 unless Defendant can show that the appropriate supplemental responses were served before the motion to compel was filed. Defendant's objections are sustained, and the motion to compel denied, as to RFPD Nos. 22-23 and Interrogatories Nos. 8, 9, 13 and 14. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 6, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT JEFFREY FELDMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part; denied in part. Plaintiff has agreed to provide supplemental responses to the outstanding RFPD's. Those supplemental responses shall be served no later than September 21, 2012. The Court notes that the only requirement of CCP §2031 is a statement similar to "All responsive, non-privilege documents" have been produced. Plaintiffs are also directed to ascertain whether they can improve the visual clarity of the documents Defendant contends are illegible. =(302/JPT)

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Defendants to provide responses to the outstanding discovery no later than September 27, 2012. Court awards sanctions to Plaintiffs in the amount of $1,250. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WONG et al VS. THE OLYMPIC CLUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Paul Wong, Teresa Canion'S Motion To Compel Defendants To File Answers To Form Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Document (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 6, 2012, Line 8, PLAINTIFFS PAUL WONG, TERESA CANION'S Motion To Compel Defendants To File Answers To Form Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Document. (Part 2 of 2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
